text:
Assistant: Decision: **remove `whoami` entirely**, fold self-identity into `who`. [...] - Attaches an `out["self"]` object with `label`, `pubkey`, `channel` (=`rec.channel_h`), `host`, `is_member` (`s.is_channel_member(&rec.channel_h, &instance.pubkey)`), `working`, `status` (=`rec.title`), `pending` (read-only `drain_pending_for_session`), `created_at`, and a RAW `session_id` for correlation. **No `codename` field.**
 [...] - Deleted `display_row_agent_name`, `agent_name_counts_for_scope`, and `agent_count_key` (the entire codename-disambiguation apparatus).
- `render_who_row` and `render_who_markdown_row` now render `row.slug` directly (concurrent same-agent instances carry distinct ordinal slugs, e.g. `haiku`/`haiku1`); their `name_counts`/`split_counts_by_project` params and the corresponding setup in `render_who_once`/`render_who_plain` were removed. Removed the `session_codename` reference. [...] - Deleted `render_whoami`; replaced it with `render_self_header(v) -> Option<String>` driven by `out["self"]` — a concise leading line `You are **{label}** on **{channel}** ({host}).` plus `pubkey … · status … · member yes/no · N pending`. Never shows a codename or raw session id.
- Wired the header into both `who` (printed before the fabric block) and `who_live` (prepended to the live fabric screen).
 [...] - **whoami removed entirely**; self-identity folded into `who` (a `self` block + header).
